content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain support implemented and tested (830 tests pass, mypy/flake8 clean). Summary of changes so you can layer on top safely: (1) Added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext wrapper functions near the existing _make_new_pgettext/_make_new_npgettext (right before 'class InternationalizationExtension'). (2) _install() now also extracts dgettext/dngettext attrs from the translations object and passes them to _install_callables. (3) _install_callables() signature gained dgettext=None, dngettext=None params; wraps them with the new helpers in newstyle mode, falls back to stdlib gettext.dgettext/dngettext when not provided, and registers them in environment.globals. (4) _uninstall() now also pops 'dgettext'/'dngettext' keys. (5) parse(): added a local  alongside ; inside the assign-branch of the parameter loop, added a special case: if token.value == 'domain', validate it isn't specified twice (TemplateAssertionError 'translation domain specified twice.'), validate the parsed expression is a nodes.Const str (else TemplateAssertionError 'the translation domain must be a string literal constant...'), store domain, skip_if('comma') to consume an optional trailing comma, then . (6) The call to self._make_node(...) now passes  as a 7th positional arg. (7) _make_node() gained a  param (used with explicit  /  annotations to keep mypy happy) and generates dgettext(domain, message)/dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n) calls when domain is set, else falls back to gettext/ngettext as before. None of this touches _parse_block, _trim_whitespace, _extract, or extract_from_ast/babel_extract - those are untouched and safe for you to extend if your feature needs them. Go ahead and read the current file state and add your note/created/modified params on top.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain support implemented and tested (830 tests pass, mypy/flake8 clean). Summary so you can layer on top safely: (1) Added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext wrapper functions right before class InternationalizationExtension, next to the existing _make_new_pgettext/_make_new_npgettext. (2) _install() now also extracts dgettext/dngettext attrs from the translations object and passes them to _install_callables. (3) _install_callables() signature gained dgettext=None, dngettext=None params; wraps them in newstyle mode, falls back to stdlib gettext.dgettext/dngettext when not provided, registers them in environment.globals. (4) _uninstall() now also pops dgettext/dngettext keys. (5) parse(): added a local domain variable (Optional[str], default None) alongside trimmed; inside the assign-branch of the parameter loop, added a special case for token.value == 'domain': validates not specified twice (TemplateAssertionError, message: translation domain specified twice), validates the parsed expression is a string constant (else TemplateAssertionError, message: the translation domain must be a string literal constant...), stores domain, skips an optional trailing comma, then continues the loop. (6) The call to self._make_node(...) now passes domain as a 7th positional arg. (7) _make_node() gained a domain param (Optional[str], default None), with explicit type annotations for func/args to keep mypy happy, and generates dgettext(domain, message) / dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n) calls when domain is set, else falls back to gettext/ngettext as before. I did not touch _parse_block, _trim_whitespace, _extract, extract_from_ast, or babel_extract - safe for you to extend if needed. Please read the current file state before adding your note/created/modified params.
